_comment: MesseBuddy SCRUM configuration. Edit this file to adapt field names, status values, and agent behaviour to your project. The agent reads this at runtime — changes take effect immediately without restarting the server.

product.name: MesseBuddy
product.vision: Help new Messe München employees integrate quickly by connecting them with experienced mentors.
product.product_goal: Enable any new employee to find and communicate with a buddy within their first week.

sprint_goal._comment: One sentence describing the outcome this sprint delivers. Set during Sprint Planning. Used to evaluate sprint success at Sprint Review.

field_names._comment: Exact names of GitHub Projects v2 custom fields. Case-sensitive. Must match what is visible in the GitHub UI.

status_values._comment: Exact option names for the Status single-select field. Case-sensitive.

priority._comment: Priority uses MoSCoW — a single-select field, not a numeric one. Options are ordered from highest to lowest priority. The agent uses array index as rank (index 0 = highest).

item_types._comment: Valid values for the Type single-select field.

item_id._comment: Tasks use a flat T-prefix to remain sprint-agnostic. Sprint association is tracked via the Sprint field, not the ID.

sprint._comment: duration_days: set to null to derive sprint length from the actual GitHub iteration dates (recommended for variable-length sprints like this project). Set to a number only if all sprints are the same length.

autonomy._comment: Controls how aggressively the agent acts on ambiguous input. 'conservative' = confirm everything. 'standard' = confirm bulk and natural-language-triggered writes. 'full' = autonomous for unambiguous single-item writes.

criteria: Written in user story format with a clear who / what / why
Acceptance criteria clearly defined and agreed by PO + team
Estimated by the development team (story points agreed via Planning Poker)
Dependencies identified and either resolved or de-risked
UI/UX approach noted (wireframe, sketch, or written description)
Technical approach understood at a high level by the whole team
Item is small enough to be completed within a single sprint
Code written and self-reviewed by the author
Code reviewed and approved by at least one other team member
No new linting or static analysis warnings introduced
Feature branch merged into the main/develop branch
Feature functions as described in all acceptance criteria
Basic smoke test passed in the shared staging environment
No critical bugs introduced (checked against existing features)
Inline code comments added where logic is non-obvious
Any new API endpoint documented in the README or API doc file
Decision log updated if a significant architectural choice was made
Feature deployed to the shared staging environment
Team has verified it runs correctly in staging (not just locally)
Acceptance criteria verified by Peter Tubak (or designated team member) in Sprint Review
Code committed to the shared GitHub repository
No uncommitted changes left on developer machines
Commit message follows agreed format: [US-XX] Short description

story_points._comment: Items estimated above max_points_per_item should be split before entering a sprint.

impediment._comment: Any impediment open beyond threshold is escalated to Scrum Master and flagged in the daily report.
